ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR AT THE MOVING PARTY'S REQUEST. DEFENDANT'S ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION (GENERAL NEGLIGENCE EXCLUDING NEGLIGENT FAILURE TO WARN, STRICT LIABILITY DESIGN AND MANUFACTURING DEFECT) IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SATISFIED ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION OF MAKING A PRIMA FACIE CASE THAT IT IS ENTITLED TO PREVAIL ON ITS GOVERNMENT CONTRACTOR DEFENSE AS TO NON-DUTY TO WARN CLAIMS AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE ON THIS DEFENSE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: KIM RICHARDSON OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 5915

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-27-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Felicia Fung of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4175), appearing for defendant / moving party Metalclad Insulation Corporation
James P. Nevin of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Dominic Elia

RULING:
DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation's motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party's request. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication (general negligence excluding negligent failure to warn, strict liability design and manufacturing defect) is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to non-duty to warn claims and plaintiff has not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue on this defense. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kim Richardson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 5915 (503/TLJ)
